title: Check if a string is anagram of another

content.problemStatement:
Given two strings, your task is to check if they are anagrams of each other. Two strings are considered anagrams if by rearranging the letters of one string, we can get the other string. Your program should be able to read two strings from the input and output "YES" if they are anagrams of each other, and "NO" otherwise.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines.

The first line of each test case contains the first string, AAA.
The second line of each test case contains the second string, BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, print "YES" if the two strings are anagrams of each other; otherwise, print "NO". Each output should be in a new line.
constraints: A and B contain only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains string A. The second line contains string B. Both strings consist only of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print "YES" if A and B are anagrams of each other, otherwise print "NO". Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 'listen' and 'silent' have identical character frequencies.\nTest case 2: 'programming' and 'margorpign' differ in the count of 'm'.\nTest case 3: 'cat' and 'tac' are anagrams.
First pair differs, second pair are anagrams.
Both strings contain two of each character a, b, c.

solution.approach: Two strings are anagrams iff they have the same length and the frequency of each character (a‑z) is identical. For each test case we first compare lengths. If they differ we output NO. Otherwise we maintain an array cnt[26] initialized to zero, increment for each character of A and decrement for each character of B. After processing both strings, if all entries of cnt are zero the strings are anagrams, else they are not. This runs in O(|A|+|B|) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space (26 integers).
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case where L = |A| + |B|, total O( Σ (|A|+|B|) ) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a fixed size array of 26 integers is used.

code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
idx=1
out=[]
for _ in range(T):
    A=data[idx].strip()
    B=data[idx+1].strip()
    idx+=2
    if len(A)!=len(B):
        out.append('NO')
        continue
    cnt=[0]*26
    for ch in A:
        cnt[ord(ch)-97]+=1
    for ch in B:
        cnt[ord(ch)-97]-=1
    out.append('YES' if all(c==0 for c in cnt) else 'NO')
print('\n'.join(out))
